description:

So how did he feel when he finally did cross the finish line and take the chequered flag first?

The guys discuss Lewis Hamilton’s battles with Nico Rosberg at Mercedes and Fernando Alonso at McLaren, the major flashpoints during Sebastian Vettel and Mark Webber’s time at Red Bull, and the politics between Ayrton Senna and Alain Prost at McLaren.

title: William's F1 2026 Collapse Explained
description:
Williams were supposed to be one of Formula 1’s big success stories in 2026.

After a fifth-place finish, a major title sponsor, and a huge hiring drive, this was meant to be the year James Vowles’ rebuild finally paid off. Instead, the FW48 arrived overweight, undertested, and operationally exposed.

In this video, I break down why Williams’ 2026 collapse was about far more than just one bad car. From the missed Barcelona shakedown, to the team’s reliance on simulation, to manufacturing bottlen

title: F1’s Biggest SCANDAL 😳 Crashgate Explained! #F1 #SingaporeGP #Controversy #formula1 #f1shorts
description:
🏁 Welcome to Curious F1 Girl — where we decode Formula 1 like never before.

From iconic rivalries to last-lap drama, every story in Formula One has layers… and we break them down.

The 2008 Singapore Grand Prix witnessed one of the biggest scandals in Formula One history.

Known as “Crashgate,” this controversy involved a deliberate crash that completely changed the outcome of the race.

A safety car at the perfect moment… a strategy turned upside down… and a championship ultimately decided by

title: What Makes The 'Perfect' F1 Driver? | F1 Nation Podcast
description:
Verstappen's mentality? Schumacher's teamwork? Alonso's intelligence? Tsunoda's physique? Tom Clarkson is joined by former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer and IndyCar race winner James Hinchcliffe to build the ‘perfect’ F1 driver.

They debate the key attributes needed to become the best in the pinnacle of motorsport and which drivers from past and present are closest to perfection.

Plus, the guys chat about the possible driver market ramifications if Max Verstappen does leave Red Bull Racing and they

title: F1 Podium Finishes But The Drivers Get Progressively Younger
description:
Over the decades, Formula 1 drivers have gone from gentleman drivers in their 40s and 50s to fresh-faced rookies, barely old enough to hold a driving license. In this video, we count down through the years, as F1's podium scorers get younger and younger!

title: The F1 Safety Car Driver! Bernd Maylander: 500 Races In The Safety Car | F1 Beyond The Grid
description:
At this year’s season-opening Australian Grand Prix, Bernd Maylander reached a remarkable milestone of 500 races as the FIA Safety Car Driver.

Speaking to Tom Clarkson, Bernd reflects on how the Safety Car and his job have evolved since his first race back in 2000 and some of the most iconic races he’s been involved in over the last 25 years.

He talks through what a Grand Prix weekend looks like for him, the role his co-driver Richard Darker plays and some of the challenges they face – like pi

title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP RACE analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Kimi Antonelli didn't make a great start from the Suzuka pole but he recovered well before taking full advantage of a timely Safety Car on lap 22: back in front at the restart, with Oscar Piastri's McLaren-Mercedes now in his mirrors, the young Italian then disappeared into the Japanese sunset, setting fastest lap as he secured his second win in a row. Oscar duly finished a gritty P2, beating Charles Leclerc's Ferrari to the line by 1.5sec, while George Russell could finish only P4 for Mercedes

title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP QUALIFYING Analysis
description:
2026 F1 Japanese GP QUALIFYING Analysis by Martin Villari

Kimi Antonelli takes pole for Mercedes in Japan/Suzuka ahead of Tomorrow's Race! The Mclaren boys did well and so did Charles Lec but a mistake on the exit of Spoon cost him 2 tenths on his final run!

Who will win tomorrow!?

CHAPTERS:
00:00 Introduction
00:20 Degner 1 and Degner 2 (LiCo V Throttle) Analysis
06:51 Quali Thoughts
09:35 Mclaren Telemetry
11:10 Ferrari Telemetry
12:05 Your Comments

title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis
description:
2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis. 

Oscar Piastri tops Friday practice at Suzuka, leading both Mercedes drivers in a session that’s already exposing the circuit’s usual high-speed challenges. McLaren look especially strong through the Esses and into Degner, with Piastri’s balance allowing him to carry consistent minimum speed through the long-direction changes.

George Russell and Kimi Antonelli follow closely behind, with Mercedes showing solid one-lap pace but probably saving perf
